description: Kepler derived three fundamental laws describing planetary motion from Tycho Brahe's observational data.

name: tea-introduction
description: Dutch traders brought Chinese tea to Europe, beginning a trade that would reshape global commerce and culture.

name: galileo-telescopic-observations
description: Galileo published Sidereus Nuncius describing telescopic discoveries including Jupiter's moons and lunar mountains.

name: galileo-telescope-discoveries
description: Galileo's telescopic observations provided key evidence for the Copernican heliocentric model of the solar system.

name: joseon-korean-medicine
description: Korean physician Heo Jun published the Dongeuibogam, a comprehensive medical encyclopedia that became a key East Asian medical text.

name: kepler-second-law
description: Johannes Kepler published his first two laws of planetary motion in Astronomia Nova, showing planets sweep equal areas in equal times.

name: plymouth-colony-pilgrims
description: English Pilgrims aboard the Mayflower established Plymouth Colony in present-day Massachusetts.

name: harvey-blood-circulation
description: William Harvey published De Motu Cordis, demonstrating that blood circulates through the body pumped by the heart.

name: descartes-analytical-geometry
description: René Descartes published La Géométrie, uniting algebra and geometry through coordinate systems.

name: barometer-invention
description: Evangelista Torricelli invented the mercury barometer, proving atmospheric pressure and the existence of vacuum.

name: first-barometer
description: Torricelli created the first mercury barometer in Florence, measuring atmospheric pressure for the first time.

name: pascal-vacuum-experiments
description: Blaise Pascal's Puy-de-Dôme experiment confirmed atmospheric pressure decreases with altitude, advancing vacuum science.

name: dutch-naval-dominance
description: The Dutch Republic became the foremost maritime power, controlling global trade routes and dominating European shipping.

name: thermometer-development
description: Ferdinand II de' Medici developed sealed liquid-in-glass thermometers, improving on earlier open-tube designs.

name: bleaching-techniques-development
description: European textile workers refined bleaching methods using buttermilk and sunlight to whiten linen and cloth.

name: pascal-probability-theory
description: Blaise Pascal and Pierre de Fermat exchanged letters founding probability theory through the problem of points.

name: optical-lens-advancement
description: Advances in glass grinding and polishing enabled improved lenses for telescopes and microscopes across Europe.

name: boyle-gas-laws
description: Robert Boyle published his law showing the inverse relationship between gas pressure and volume.

name: boyle-law-gases
description: Boyle demonstrated that gas volume is inversely proportional to pressure at constant temperature.

name: philosophical-transactions
description: The Royal Society launched Philosophical Transactions, the world's first peer-reviewed scientific journal.

name: hooke-microscopy
description: Robert Hooke published Micrographia, coining the term 'cell' and revealing the microscopic world through detailed illustrations.

name: newton-calculus
description: Isaac Newton developed his method of fluxions (calculus) during his annus mirabilis at Woolsthorpe Manor.

name: spectroscopy-prism
description: Newton used a glass prism to split white light into its spectrum, revealing that light is composed of colors.

name: blood-transfusion
description: Jean-Baptiste Denys performed the first documented blood transfusion from a lamb to a human patient in Paris.

name: leeuwenhoek-microscope
description: Antonie van Leeuwenhoek crafted powerful single-lens microscopes and became the first to observe microorganisms.

name: joseon-silhak-agricultural
description: Korean Silhak scholars promoted practical learning and agricultural reform during the Joseon Dynasty.

name: first-microscope
description: Leeuwenhoek reported his first observations of 'animalcules' (microorganisms) to the Royal Society using his handmade microscopes.

name: huygens-light-theory
description: Christiaan Huygens proposed that light travels as waves, explaining reflection and refraction phenomena.

name: leibniz-binary-system
description: Gottfried Wilhelm Leibniz published his binary number system, using only 0 and 1, laying groundwork for modern computing.

name: newton-leibniz-calculus
description: Leibniz published his calculus notation in 1684, while Newton had developed his version independently in the 1660s.

name: newton-gravitation-law
description: Newton published his universal law of gravitation in the Principia, explaining planetary orbits and earthly motion.

name: newton-principia-published
description: Newton's Principia Mathematica laid the foundation for classical mechanics with laws of motion and universal gravitation.

name: newton-gravity-motion
description: Newton's three laws of motion and law of universal gravitation transformed physics and astronomy.

name: agricultural-revolution
description: Jethro Tull's seed drill and new crop rotation methods launched Britain's Agricultural Revolution.

name: coal-mining-expansion
description: British coal output surged as demand grew for heating and early industrial processes, spurring deeper mining techniques.

name: selective-breeding-animals
description: British farmers began systematic selective breeding of cattle and sheep, increasing meat and wool yields.

name: hydrochloric-acid-production
description: German chemists refined methods for producing hydrochloric acid, advancing chemical industry processes.

name: dye-chemistry-advances
description: European chemists developed more colorfast dyes for textiles, improving on centuries-old natural dyeing techniques.

name: long-distance-travel-improvement
description: Improvements in road networks, ship design, and navigation reduced journey times across continents and oceans.

name: halley-comet-orbital
description: Edmond Halley predicted the periodic return of a comet using Newton's laws, later confirmed in 1758.

name: fahrenheit-temperature-scale
description: Daniel Gabriel Fahrenheit developed the mercury thermometer and standardized temperature scale still used in the US.

name: achromatic-lens
description: Chester Moore Hall invented the achromatic lens, combining crown and flint glass to reduce chromatic aberration.

name: euler-graph-theory
description: Leonhard Euler solved the Seven Bridges of Königsberg problem, founding the mathematical field of graph theory.

name: euler-mathematics
description: Euler published Introductio in Analysin Infinitorum, advancing analysis, number theory, and mathematical notation.

name: celsius-temperature-scale
description: Anders Celsius proposed a 100-degree temperature scale based on the freezing and boiling points of water.

name: turbine-wheel-concept
description: Early turbine wheel designs emerged in France as engineers explored rotary power from water and steam.

name: natural-rubber-gathering
description: Charles Marie de La Condamine sent rubber samples from South America to Europe, sparking scientific interest in latex.

name: black-phlogiston-heat
description: Joseph Black discovered latent heat and specific heat, challenging the phlogiston theory of combustion.

name: sextant-refinement
description: John Bird built improved sextants for the Royal Navy, enabling more precise celestial navigation at sea.

name: cotton-spinning-jenny
description: James Hargreaves invented the spinning jenny, enabling one worker to operate multiple spindles simultaneously.

name: veterinary-school
description: Claude Bourgelat founded the first veterinary school in Lyon, France, establishing veterinary medicine as a profession.

name: bougainville-circumnavigation
description: Louis Antoine de Bougainville led the first French circumnavigation, exploring Tahiti and the Pacific Islands.

name: cook-pacific-voyages
description: Captain James Cook embarked on the first of three Pacific voyages, mapping coastlines and advancing navigation.

name: arkwright-water-frame
description: Richard Arkwright patented the water frame, a water-powered spinning machine that industrialized cotton production.

name: watt-separate-condenser
description: James Watt patented the separate condenser for steam engines, dramatically improving fuel efficiency.

name: lagrange-number-theory
description: Joseph-Louis Lagrange proved that every positive integer can be expressed as the sum of four squares.

name: zanzibar-clove-cultivation
description: The Sultan of Zanzibar established clove plantations that made the island the world's leading spice exporter.

name: lavoisier-oxygen-combustion
description: Antoine Lavoisier named oxygen and demonstrated its role in combustion, overthrowing phlogiston theory.

name: priestley-oxygen-isolation
description: Joseph Priestley isolated oxygen by heating mercuric oxide, calling it 'dephlogisticated air.'

name: herschel-discovers-uranus
description: William Herschel discovered Uranus, the first planet found by telescope and the first since antiquity.

name: cotton-power-loom
description: Edmund Cartwright patented the power loom, mechanizing weaving and transforming the textile industry.

name: coulomb-electrostatics
description: Charles-Augustin de Coulomb published his law describing the force between electric charges using a torsion balance.

name: lavoisier-chemical-nomenclature
description: Lavoisier co-authored a systematic chemical naming system that replaced alchemical terminology with rational names.

name: lagrange-mechanics
description: Lagrange published Mécanique Analytique, reformulating classical mechanics using calculus of variations.

name: chemistry-lavoisier
description: Lavoisier published Traité Élémentaire de Chimie, establishing modern chemistry with a list of elements and conservation of mass.

name: metric-system-france
description: France officially adopted the metric system, standardizing measurement based on decimal units.

name: jenner-vaccination-smallpox
description: Edward Jenner demonstrated that cowpox inoculation protected against smallpox, pioneering vaccination.

name: volta-electric-battery
description: Alessandro Volta invented the voltaic pile, the first true electric battery producing a steady current.

name: high-pressure-steam
description: Richard Trevithick developed high-pressure steam engines, enabling more powerful and compact designs.

name: breech-loading-mechanism
description: Early breech-loading firearms allowed loading from the rear, increasing firing rate over muzzle-loaders.

name: young-double-slit-experiment
description: Thomas Young's double-slit experiment demonstrated light interference, providing evidence for wave theory of light.

name: percussion-cap-invention
description: Scottish clergyman Alexander Forsyth patented the percussion ignition system, replacing unreliable flintlocks.

name: atom-dalton
description: John Dalton published his atomic theory, proposing that elements consist of indivisible atoms with specific weights.

name: avogadro-molecular-hypothesis
description: Amedeo Avogadro proposed that equal volumes of gases at equal temperature and pressure contain equal numbers of molecules.

name: cuvier-paleontology
description: Georges Cuvier established paleontology through fossil studies and demonstrated that species could go extinct.

name: fresnel-wave-optics
description: Augustin-Jean Fresnel mathematically formalized the wave theory of light, explaining diffraction patterns.

name: smith-stratigraphy
description: William Smith published the first geological map of England, establishing stratigraphy using fossil identification.

name: magnetism-electromagnetism
description: Hans Christian Ørsted discovered that electric current produces a magnetic field, linking electricity and magnetism.

name: ampere-electromagnetism
description: André-Marie Ampère formulated the mathematical theory of electromagnetism and the right-hand rule for current.

name: carnot-thermodynamics
description: Sadi Carnot published Reflections on the Motive Power of Fire, establishing the theoretical limits of heat engines.

name: railway-locomotive-stephenson
description: George Stephenson's Rocket won the Rainhill Trials, proving steam locomotives viable for passenger railways.

name: lyell-uniformitarianism
description: Charles Lyell published Principles of Geology, arguing Earth was shaped by slow, continuous natural processes.

name: faraday-electromagnetic-induction
description: Michael Faraday discovered electromagnetic induction, showing that changing magnetic fields generate electric current.

name: cell-structure
description: Schleiden and Schwann proposed cell theory, establishing that all living organisms are composed of cells.

name: joule-heat-energy
description: James Prescott Joule demonstrated the mechanical equivalent of heat, showing energy conversion between forms.

name: vulcanization-goodyear
description: Charles Goodyear discovered vulcanization, making rubber durable and heat-resistant through sulfur treatment.

name: faraday-electromagnetic-field
description: Faraday introduced the concept of electromagnetic fields and demonstrated the magneto-optical (Faraday) effect.

name: anesthesia-ether
description: William Morton publicly demonstrated ether anesthesia during surgery at Massachusetts General Hospital.

name: joule-mechanical-equivalent-heat
description: Joule precisely measured the mechanical equivalent of heat using his famous paddle wheel apparatus.

name: semmelweis-childbed-fever
description: Ignaz Semmelweis showed that handwashing with chlorinated lime drastically reduced maternal mortality in hospitals.

name: hooker-plant-distribution
description: Joseph Dalton Hooker studied Antarctic and global flora, pioneering the field of plant biogeography.

name: rubber-cultivation-begins
description: Henry Wickham smuggled rubber seeds from Brazil to Kew Gardens, enabling plantation cultivation in Southeast Asia.

name: clausius-entropy
description: Rudolf Clausius formulated the second law of thermodynamics and introduced the concept of entropy.

name: compound-steam-engine
description: John Elder patented the marine compound steam engine, using steam in successive cylinders for greater efficiency.

name: virchow-cellular-pathology
description: Rudolf Virchow published Cellular Pathology, establishing that all diseases arise from changes in normal cells.

name: wallace-evolution-theory
description: Alfred Russel Wallace independently conceived natural selection, prompting the joint Darwin-Wallace presentation.

name: huxley-vertebrate-anatomy
description: Thomas Henry Huxley advanced comparative vertebrate anatomy and became a leading defender of Darwin's evolution theory.

name: darwin-evolution-origin
description: Charles Darwin published On the Origin of Species, presenting natural selection as the mechanism of evolution.

name: maxwell-kinetic-theory
description: James Clerk Maxwell described gas behavior statistically, founding the kinetic theory of gases.

name: nightingale-nursing-reform
description: Florence Nightingale founded the first professional nursing school at St Thomas' Hospital in London.

name: first-weather-forecast
description: Robert FitzRoy established the first daily weather forecasts published in The Times newspaper.

name: antiseptic-surgery
description: Joseph Lister introduced carbolic acid as an antiseptic in surgery, dramatically reducing infection rates.

name: maxwell-electromagnetic-equations
description: Maxwell unified electricity, magnetism, and optics into four equations predicting electromagnetic waves.

name: inheritance-mendel
description: Gregor Mendel published his experiments on pea plants, establishing the fundamental laws of genetic inheritance.

name: haeckel-embryology
description: Ernst Haeckel published Generelle Morphologie, proposing that embryonic development recapitulates evolutionary history.

name: mendeleev-periodic-table
description: Dmitri Mendeleev arranged elements by atomic weight, predicting undiscovered elements and their properties.

name: surgery-antiseptic-system
description: Listerine was formulated as a surgical antiseptic inspired by Joseph Lister's carbolic acid methods.

name: koch-tuberculosis-bacterium
description: Robert Koch identified Mycobacterium tuberculosis as the cause of TB, establishing germ theory of disease.

name: surgery-local-anesthesia
description: Karl Koller demonstrated cocaine as the first local anesthetic for eye surgery in Vienna.

name: x-ray-discovery
description: Wilhelm Röntgen discovered X-rays, revolutionizing medical diagnosis and earning the first Nobel Prize in Physics.

name: wright-brothers-flight
description: Orville and Wilbur Wright achieved the first powered, controlled airplane flight at Kitty Hawk, North Carolina.

name: blood-clotting
description: Paul Morawitz described the classical theory of blood coagulation involving four clotting factors.

name: antibiotics
description: Alexander Fleming discovered penicillin when mold contaminated a bacterial culture, launching the antibiotic era.

name: sputnik-launch-space
description: The Soviet Union launched Sputnik 1, the first artificial satellite, igniting the Space Race with the United States.

name: recurved-bow-design
description: Central Asian nomads developed the recurved composite bow, storing more energy in a shorter design for mounted archery.

name: winnowing-tray-fan
description: Asian farmers developed winnowing trays and fans to separate grain from chaff using wind.

name: grain-storage-silos
description: Ancient Egyptians built granaries and silo structures to store surplus grain for trade and famine relief.

name: bronze-adoption-mediterranean
description: Bronze metallurgy spread across the Mediterranean, enabling stronger tools and weapons than copper alone.

name: domestication-potato-americas
description: Andean peoples domesticated the potato near Lake Titicaca, creating a staple crop for high-altitude farming.

name: millet-adoption-china
description: Foxtail and broomcorn millet became major cultivated crops in northern China's Yellow River valley.

name: domestication-rice-southeast-asia
description: Rice was domesticated in the Yangtze River valley, eventually becoming the staple food for much of Asia.

name: domestication-llama-alpaca
description: Andean peoples domesticated llamas and alpacas for transport, wool, and meat in the high Andes.

name: domestication-maize-americas
description: Indigenous peoples in the Balsas River valley of Mexico domesticated teosinte into maize, the Americas' most important crop.

name: domestication-wheat-middle-east
description: Early farmers in the Fertile Crescent domesticated emmer and einkorn wheat, launching the Neolithic agricultural revolution.
